name: Chicken with Blackberry Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 whole boned and skinned chicken breast halves
1 1/4 cups fat free chicken broth, divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Press blackberries through a sieve with the back of a spoon to remove the seeds. Reserve the resulting puree (about 1/2 cup).
2. Remove all visible fat from the chicken breasts. Rinse well and pat dry with paper towels. Sprinkle both sides with pepper.
3. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil until shimmering in a large, nonstick skillet. Saute chicken breasts until lightly browned on each side and mostly cooked (about 3-4 minutes per side). Remove from pan.
4. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil to the pan. Stir in shallots and mushrooms and saute for 1 minute.
5. Add 1/4 cup of the chicken broth and continue cooking until shallots are softened but not brown (about another minute).
6. Combine cornstarch with the remaining cup of chicken broth and add to the vegetables. Stir in the reserved blackberry puree. Return the chicken breasts to the pan.
7. Cook until the sauce is thickened and bubbly, and the juices run clear.
